sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- After SPACE+RIGHT: the blue cross is now a single connected piece at center (45,36) with 53 pixels, and the yellow cross is split into 4 arms.
- The blue cross moved from center (45,36) to... still (45,36)? Wait, let me check.
Initial blue cross center was around (45,36) (the intersection of the 4 arms). After SPACE+RIGHT, the blue cross is at (45,36) with 53 pixels. But wait, the initial blue cross was split into 4 parts. Let me look at the initial state more carefully.
Initial blue cross parts:
- (38,36) - top arm
- (45,29) - left arm
- (45,43) - right arm
- (52,36) - bottom arm
Center would be around (45,36).
Current blue cross: center (45,36), 53 pixels - it's the same position but now connected.
But wait, the yellow cross changed too. Initial yellow cross was at (27,21) with 45 pixels. Now it's split into 4 arms at different positions. Let me check if the yellow cross moved.
Initial yellow cross center: (27,21)
Current yellow cross arms: (21,24), (27,18), (27,30), (33,24)
The center of these 4 arms would be around (27,24). So the yellow cross center moved from (27,21) to (27,24) - that's 3 columns to the right!
So after SPACE, RIGHT moves the YELLOW cross (not the blue one). Before SPACE, RIGHT moved the BLUE cross.
This confirms: SPACE toggles which cross is controlled. Arrow keys move the controlled cross.
Now I need to figure out the goal. The gray squares with colored dots are likely targets. The goal is probably to move each cross to collect the dots of matching color.
Let me now reset to the initial state and plan a solution. First, let me go back by pressing LEFT to undo the RIGHT movement, then SPACE to toggle back.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Reset: LEFT to undo RIGHT, then SPACE to toggle back to controlling blue cross
action(['LEFT', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
